Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = 和多都美御子神社 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=対馬市}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|対馬市|Q991032}}{{jalink|豊玉町|Q11633965}}仁位桜町1416 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}、{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}} | 神体 = | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 札所等 = | 宮司 = | 別名 = | 例祭 = | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=和多都美
御子神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''和多都美御子神社'''（わたつみのみこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|対馬市|Q991032}}{{jalink|豊玉町|Q11633965}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === 元は天神社であり、和多都美御子神社との関係はなかった。{{jalink|貞和|Q1146143}}5年（{{jalink|1349年|Q6090}}）7月25日に{{jalink|筑前国|Q1047664}}{{jalink|大宰府|Q144845}}から天満宮を勧請したのが始まりであるという平山棐『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/956888 津島紀事]』（津島紀事刊行会、1917年）。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}初期に和多都美御子神社と改称したが、これは{{jalink|海神神社|Q11559099}}が{{jalink|和多都美神社|Q18457651}}を名乗ったことに起因している。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 参考文献 === * 『対馬神社誌』,長崎県対馬神社総代会,1966. 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3451609 (参照 2025-06-09) === 出典 === {{reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長崎県}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:わたつみのみこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:対馬市の歴史]] [[Category:対馬市の建築物]] [[Category:対馬島の式内社]]

Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name = Watatsumi no Miko Shrine | Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Tsushima City}} | Location = {{jalink|Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376}}{{jalink|Tsushima City|Q991032}}{{jalink|Toyotama Town|Q11633965}} 1416 Nii Sakuramachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (disputed), former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} | Established = | Main hall = | Pilgrimage designation = | Chief priest = | Alternative names = | Annual festival = | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Watatsumi
no Miko Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Watatsumi no Miko Shrine''' (わたつみのみこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376}}, {{jalink|Tsushima City|Q991032}}, {{jalink|Toyotama Town|Q11633965}}. === Overview === Originally, it was a Tenjin shrine and had no connection to Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. It is said to have begun on July 25 of the 5th year of {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1146143}} ({{jalink|1349|Q6090}}), when a Tenmangū was invited (kanjō) from {{jalink|Chikuzen Province|Q1047664}} {{jalink|Dazaifu|Q144845}}.Hirayama Tsunefusa, ‘[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/956888 Tsushima Kiji]’ (Tsushima Kiji Publication Society, 1917) In the early {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} period, it was renamed Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. This change was due to {{jalink|Kaijin Shrine|Q11559099}} adopting the name {{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine|Q18457651}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} === References === * *Tsushima Shrine Records*, Nagasaki Prefecture Tsushima Shrine Representatives Association, 1966. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3451609 (accessed 2025-06-09) === Sources === {{reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagasaki Prefecture}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Watatsuminomikojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:対馬市の歴史]] [[Category:対馬市の建築物]] [[Category:対馬島の式内社]]

Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name = Watatsumi no Miko Shrine | Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Tsushima City}} | Location = {{jalink|Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376}}{{jalink|Tsushima City|Q991032}}{{jalink|Toyotama Town|Q11633965}} 1416 Nii Sakuramachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (disputed), former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} | Established = | Main hall = | Pilgrimage designation = | Chief priest = | Alternative names = | Annual festival = | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Watatsumi
no Miko Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Watatsumi no Miko Shrine''' (わたつみのみこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376}}, {{jalink|Tsushima City|Q991032}}, {{jalink|Toyotama Town|Q11633965}}. === Overview === Originally, it was a Tenjin shrine and had no connection to Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. It is said to have begun on July 25 of the 5th year of {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1146143}} ({{jalink|1349|Q6090}}), when a Tenmangū was invited (kanjō) from {{jalink|Chikuzen Province|Q1047664}} {{jalink|Dazaifu|Q144845}}.Hirayama Tsunefusa, ‘[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/956888 Tsushima Kiji]’ (Tsushima Kiji Publication Society, 1917) In the early {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} period, it was renamed Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. This change was due to {{jalink|Kaijin Shrine|Q11559099}} adopting the name {{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine|Q18457651}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} === References === * *Tsushima Shrine Records*, Nagasaki Prefecture Tsushima Shrine Representatives Association, 1966. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3451609 (accessed 2025-06-09) === Sources === {{reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagasaki Prefecture}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Watatsuminomikojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Watatsumi-no-Miko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Watatsumi-no-Miko Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:対馬市の歴史]] [[Category:対馬市の建築物]] [[Category:対馬島の式内社]]

no Miko Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Watatsumi no Miko Shrine''' (わたつみのみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}, {{ill|lt=Tsushima City|Q991032|WD=Q991032}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotama Town|Q11633965|WD=Q11633965}}. === Overview === Originally, it was a Tenjin shrine and had no connection to Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. It is said to have begun on July 25 of the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143}} ({{ill|lt=1349|Q6090|WD=Q6090}}), when a Tenmangū was invited (kanjō) from {{ill|lt=Chikuzen Province|Q1047664|WD=Q1047664}} {{ill|lt=Dazaifu|Q144845|WD=Q144845}}.Hirayama Tsunefusa, ‘[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/956888 Tsushima Kiji]’ (Tsushima Kiji Publication Society, 1917) In the early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period, it was renamed Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. This change was due to {{ill|lt=Kaijin Shrine|Q11559099|WD=Q11559099}} adopting the name {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q18457651|WD=Q18457651}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} === References === * *Tsushima Shrine Records*, Nagasaki Prefecture Tsushima Shrine Representatives Association, 1966. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3451609 (accessed 2025-06-09) === Sources === {{reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagasaki Prefecture}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Watatsuminomikojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Watatsumi-no-Miko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Watatsumi-no-Miko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:対馬市の歴史]] [[Category:対馬市の建築物]] [[Category:対馬島の式内社]]

It is said to have begun on July 25 of the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143}} ({{ill|lt=1349|Q6090|WD=Q6090}}), when a Tenmangū was invited (kanjō) from {{ill|lt=Chikuzen Province|Q1047664|WD=Q1047664}} {{ill|lt=Dazaifu|Q144845|WD=Q144845}}.Hirayama Tsunefusa, ‘[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/956888 Tsushima Kiji]’ (Tsushima Kiji Publication Society, 1917) In the early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period, it was renamed Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. This change was due to {{ill|lt=Kaijin Shrine|Q11559099|WD=Q11559099}} adopting the name {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q18457651|WD=Q18457651}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} === References === * *Tsushima Shrine Records*, Nagasaki Prefecture Tsushima Shrine Representatives Association, 1966. wanted|cat=Tsushima City}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tsushima City|Q991032|WD=Q991032|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}}{{ill|lt=Toyotama Town|Q11633965|WD=Q11633965|1=Toyotama}} 1416 Nii Sakuramachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (disputed), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Established = | Main hall = | Pilgrimage designation = | Chief priest = | Alternative names = | Annual festival = | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Watatsumi
no Miko Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Watatsumi no Miko Shrine''' (わたつみのみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Tsushima City|Q991032|WD=Q991032|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotama Town|Q11633965|WD=Q11633965|1=Toyotama}}. === Overview === Originally, it was a Tenjin shrine and had no connection to Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. It is said to have begun on July 25 of the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143|1=Jōwa (Muromachi period)}} ({{ill|lt=1349|Q6090|WD=Q6090|1=1349}}), when a Tenmangū was invited (kanjō) from {{ill|lt=Chikuzen Province|Q1047664|WD=Q1047664|1=Chikuzen Province}} {{ill|lt=Dazaifu|Q144845|WD=Q144845|1=Dazaifu (government)}}.Hirayama Tsunefusa, ‘[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/956888 Tsushima Kiji]’ (Tsushima Kiji Publication Society, 1917) In the early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, it was renamed Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. This change was due to {{ill|lt=Kaijin Shrine|Q11559099|WD=Q11559099|1=Kaijin Shrine}} adopting the name {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q18457651|WD=Q18457651|1=Watazumi Shrine}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} === References === * *Tsushima Shrine Records*, Nagasaki Prefecture Tsushima Shrine Representatives Association, 1966. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3451609 (accessed 2025-06-09) === Sources === {{reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagasaki Prefecture}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Watatsumi-no-Miko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Watatsumi-no-Miko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:対馬市の歴史]] [[Category:対馬市の建築物]] [[Category:対馬島の式内社]]

no Miko Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Watatsumi no Miko Shrine''' (わたつみのみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Tsushima City|Q991032|WD=Q991032|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotama Town|Q11633965|WD=Q11633965|1=Toyotama}}. === Overview === Originally, it was a Tenjin shrine and had no connection to Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. It is said to have begun on July 25 of the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143|1=Jōwa (Muromachi period)}} ({{ill|lt=1349|Q6090|WD=Q6090|1=1349}}), when a Tenmangū was invited (kanjō) from {{ill|lt=Chikuzen Province|Q1047664|WD=Q1047664|1=Chikuzen Province}} {{ill|lt=Dazaifu|Q144845|WD=Q144845|1=Dazaifu (government)}}.Hirayama Tsunefusa, ‘[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/956888 Tsushima Kiji]’ (Tsushima Kiji Publication Society, 1917) In the early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, it was renamed Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. This change was due to {{ill|lt=Kaijin Shrine|Q11559099|WD=Q11559099|1=Kaijin Shrine}} adopting the name {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q18457651|WD=Q18457651|1=Watazumi Shrine}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} === References === * *Tsushima Shrine Records*, Nagasaki Prefecture Tsushima Shrine Representatives Association, 1966. no Miko Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Watatsumi no Miko Shrine''' (わたつみのみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Tsushima City|Q991032|WD=Q991032|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotama Town|Q11633965|WD=Q11633965|1=Toyotama}}. === Overview === Originally, it was a Tenjin shrine and had no connection to Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. It is said to have begun on July 25 of the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143|1=Jōwa (Muromachi period)}} ({{ill|lt=1349|Q6090|WD=Q6090|1=1349}}), when a Tenmangū was invited (kanjō) from {{ill|lt=Chikuzen Province|Q1047664|WD=Q1047664|1=Chikuzen Province}} {{ill|lt=Dazaifu|Q144845|WD=Q144845|1=Dazaifu (government)}}.Hirayama Tsunefusa, ‘[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/956888 Tsushima Kiji]’ (Tsushima Kiji Publication Society, 1917) In the early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, it was renamed Watatsumi no Miko Shrine. This change was due to {{ill|lt=Kaijin Shrine|Q11559099|WD=Q11559099|1=Kaijin Shrine}} adopting the name {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q18457651|WD=Q18457651|1=Watazumi Shrine}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} === References === * *Tsushima Shrine Records*, Nagasaki Prefecture Tsushima Shrine Representatives Association, 1966.
